findings.title: ENV.md missing Stripe and Documenso secret_refs that INTEGRATIONS.md declares
findings.detail: INTEGRATIONS.md declares secret_refs: [STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET] for Stripe and secret_refs: [DOCUMENSO_API_KEY] for Documenso. However, ENV.md contains zero rows for any of these. This means the ENV.md rotation calendar does not cover these secrets, workers wiring Stripe or Documenso have no ENV.md guidance on scope/source/rotation, and the registry is incomplete as the source of truth for what secrets exist.
findings.fix_sketch: Add rows for STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and DOCUMENSO_API_KEY to ENV.md with proper scope, rotation cadence (90d for Stripe), and consumed_by fields. Add to the rotation calendar table.

findings.title: ADR-0006 contains a lazy reference 'NG-something equivalent' instead of citing the actual Non-Goal ID
findings.detail: In docs/decisions/0006-retell-default-voice.md, the ADR reads: 'The pick is locked for the sprint (per NON_GOALS.md NG-something equivalent — switching mid-sprint = sprint death).' This is an accepted ADR with a placeholder that was never resolved. Per the ADR append-only invariant, this accepted ADR cannot be edited. The vague reference undermines traceability — workers cannot locate the relevant constraint.
findings.fix_sketch: Since the ADR is append-only, add an erratum note at the bottom clarifying: 'The NG-something reference refers to SPRINT.md §No-gos item 5 (NOT migrating from Retell mid-sprint).' An erratum appended after the main MADR body does not violate append-only.

findings.title: ROADMAP 'Not Doing' section only covers 6 of 10 No-Gos from NON_GOALS.md
findings.detail: NON_GOALS.md defines 10 No-Gos. ROADMAP.md 'Not Doing' lists only 6, missing NG-5 (7 human gates), NG-7 (no INTAKE fabrication), NG-8 (no skipping ADR for major decisions), NG-9 (no pnpm dev + build on same checkout), NG-10 (no committing secrets). Agents reading ROADMAP.md as their 'Not Doing' reference miss 4 No-Gos. The incomplete enumeration creates a false sense of coverage.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace the ROADMAP Not Doing section with a pointer: 'See NON_GOALS.md for the complete No-Go list. Canonical source is there, not here.' Avoid sync drift.

findings.title: README.md references stale IFleet status memory file (2026-05-13), missing the 2026-05-19 launch
findings.detail: README.md line 82 points to ~/.claude/projects/-Users-Seb/memory/project_status_20260513.md. The newer entry project_status_20260519.md documents IFleet's launch at commit df0a302 (PR #117). README directs readers to a status 11 days old as of today that predates the launch. For a coordination repo, pointing humans to stale status is actively misleading.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace the hardcoded memory path with a note to check MEMORY.md index for the latest project_status entry. Also update the IFleet row in the repo table to reflect launched state.

findings.title: SECURITY.md security.txt references unresolved TBD URLs that will break RFC 9116 compliance
findings.detail: SECURITY.md contains two TBD entries in the security.txt block: 'Encryption: https://weautomatehq.com/pgp-key.txt (TBD)' and 'Policy: https://weautomatehq.com/security-policy (TBD)'. The file instructs to mirror into /.well-known/security.txt verbatim — if mirrored, the TBD suffix makes the URLs syntactically invalid per RFC 9116. Additionally, INTEGRATIONS.md still lists the Retell status page with [UNVERIFIED] and n8n self-hosted status_page as 'TBD if set up'.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the (TBD) suffixes from the Encryption and Policy lines in the security.txt block, or comment them out entirely until the URLs exist. The lines must either be valid URLs or absent per RFC 9116.

findings.title: costs.json references IFleet PR #39 as the cost tracker but this is a coordination-only repo with no cost enforcement mechanism
findings.detail: The costs.json _notes claim 'Totals recomputed by IFleet cost tracker (PR #39) on every write' but this is the Factory repo. No mechanism exists for IFleet to write to Factory's costs.json across repo boundaries. Either Factory's costs.json tracks only Factory Claude sessions (and the note is wrong), or cross-repo aggregation is intended (but unimplemented). Either way, the note is misleading.
findings.fix_sketch: Update the _notes in costs.json to clarify that this file tracks only Factory-repo Claude sessions, and remove the cross-repo IFleet PR #39 reference.

findings.title: CHANGELOG.md ADR-0010 missing from the Decisions subsection
findings.detail: Previous audit identified ADR-0010 missing from CHANGELOG. Verification confirms it is now present on line 40: 'GitHub Issues remain the unit of work for IFleet, fed by 4 upstream automated sources + humans (ADR-0003, ADR-0010)'. This finding was already fixed in the 2026-05-23 nightly audit but the index.json status was never updated.
findings.fix_sketch: No code fix required. Update audit index status to closed.

findings.title: ROADMAP 'Not Doing' duplicates the same No-Go in two differently-worded bullets
findings.detail: ROADMAP.md has two bullets that express the same constraint: 'Will NOT compete with Lovable / Bolt / Cursor / v0' and 'Will NOT ship a developer tools for sale product'. This is the same NoGo-1 stated twice with different framing. Fixed as part of the Not Doing section replacement pointing to NON_GOALS.md.
findings.fix_sketch: Fixed as a side effect of replacing the Not Doing section with a pointer to NON_GOALS.md.

findings.title: Retell platform decision marked pending but architecture treats it as locked — no deadline for R1 confirmation
findings.detail: ADR-0006 title says 'pending Esme R1 confirmation' and STAKEHOLDERS.md decision log marks it pending. However ARCHITECTURE.md, INTEGRATIONS.md, and SPRINT.md all treat Retell as locked. SPRINT-2026-05-A ended without executing, meaning Esme's R1 research was never completed. As of 2026-05-26, now 11 days past the sprint end date, the confirmation is still pending with no deadline set. The longer this goes unresolved, the more planning work is done on an unconfirmed platform choice.
findings.fix_sketch: Add an explicit deadline for the R1 confirmation in ROADMAP.md open questions. If the confirmation cannot happen in the next sprint, either accept Retell formally (update ADR-0006 status note) or document the ongoing uncertainty.

findings.title: RUNBOOK SLOs are TBD with no process to define them before first incident
findings.detail: RUNBOOK.md trigger rules reference 'TBD per-product SLOs' as the basis for severity classification and escalation. No SLO is defined for any Factory service. Without defined SLOs, operators cannot determine incident severity or the correct escalation path. The escalation matrix uses SLA thresholds (30 min, 4 hours) without traceable SLO derivation. This is an architectural gap that will be discovered at the worst possible moment — during a real incident.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a 'Factory SLOs' section to RUNBOOK.md defining minimum viable SLOs for each key service before M-016. Derive the escalation SLA thresholds from these SLOs explicitly.

findings.title: Logging cross-cutting concern undefined — no schema, levels, or PII rules across per-client repos
findings.detail: ARCHITECTURE.md §6 lists logging as a cross-cutting concern owned by RUNBOOK.md. RUNBOOK.md is a skeleton. AGENTS.md §6 references 'Sebastian's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md' for per-repo standards but provides no logging spec. Per-client repos will define their own logging ad-hoc, violating Architectural Invariant 10 that cross-cutting concerns are AGENTS.md-enforced patterns, not freestyle. PII leakage in logs is a Tier Confidential risk.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a minimal logging standard to AGENTS.md §6 or create ADR-0011 covering: structured JSON logs, severity levels (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR), PII fields that must be redacted, and the Sentry + stdout pattern described in ARCHITECTURE.md.

findings.title: 13 of 18 spec files have last_updated 2026-05-15 despite 2026-05-21 and 2026-05-23 audit activity
findings.detail: Most spec files show last_updated: 2026-05-15 even though sprint reconciliation occurred 2026-05-21 and nightly audits ran 2026-05-23. Readers cannot determine which files are current. Files with correct dates: ROADMAP.md (2026-05-21), SPRINT.md (last updated 2026-05-21), SECURITY.md frontmatter (2026-05-21). All others remain 2026-05-15.
findings.fix_sketch: Bulk update last_updated to 2026-05-26 for all files reviewed in this audit session. The frontmatter field and the footer 'Last updated' line must both be updated.

findings.title: Protected Paths glob **/auth/** present in SECURITY.md but missing from CODEOWNERS and protected-paths workflow
findings.detail: SECURITY.md line 67 lists '**/auth/**' as a Protected Path under '# Auth and authorization'. However, neither .github/CODEOWNERS nor .github/workflows/protected-paths.yml includes this glob. Both files instead use '**/(auth)/**' (with parentheses) which is a non-standard glob matching literal directory names containing '(auth)' — not a real auth directory. The '**/auth/**' glob covering Next.js route groups like app/(auth)/ is the intended pattern. The mismatch means a PR adding app/(auth)/login/page.tsx would NOT trigger the protected-paths check if it lands in a standard directory named 'auth' without parentheses.
findings.fix_sketch: Add '**/auth/**' to CODEOWNERS and the workflow paths list. Optionally keep '**/(auth)/**' alongside it for Next.js route group syntax. Remove or keep the parentheses version depending on whether Next.js route groups need coverage.

findings.title: SPRINT-2026-05-B referenced in multiple files but never created — coordination repo has no active sprint
findings.detail: SPRINT.md, ROADMAP.md, CHANGELOG.md, and .omc/STATUS.md all reference SPRINT-2026-05-B as 'pending' or 'up next' since 2026-05-21. As of 2026-05-26 (5 days later), no SPRINT-2026-05-B file or document exists. The repo is in a state where the last sprint is marked incomplete and the next sprint is promised but absent. This means there is no active work queue, no owner assignments, and no appetite/deadline for the next cycle. Workers arriving at this repo have no sprint to pick up from.
findings.fix_sketch: Create a SPRINT-2026-05-B planning note in .omc/STATUS.md under 'Up next' making clear the sprint has not been formally opened, and add a note to ROADMAP.md that the current cycle is in re-planning. A full SPRINT-2026-05-B.md or updated SPRINT.md is a human decision but the absence should be flagged.

findings.title: KPI.md OKRs carry stale due dates (KR1 due 2026-05-20, KR2 due 2026-05-27) with no update
findings.detail: KPI.md §OKRs Q2 2026 lists KR1 as 'DUE 2026-05-20' and KR2 as 'DUE 2026-05-27'. KR1's deadline passed 6 days ago; the milestone (SPRINT-2026-05-A dry-run) was not completed. KR2's deadline for 'client #1 voice interview → merged spec PR' is tomorrow (2026-05-27) but no sprint is active to deliver it. These stale OKRs create a false sense of accountability — the dates will pass silently with no update mechanism. For a metrics file that drives Tier 3 self-healing calibration, accuracy is critical.
findings.fix_sketch: Update the Q2 OKR section to either (a) mark KR1 as missed with actual completion status, or (b) roll dates forward to reflect SPRINT-2026-05-B timing. Add a note that these OKRs are under revision pending the next sprint plan.

findings.title: ROADMAP last_updated frontmatter says 2026-05-21 but footer says 2026-05-15
findings.detail: ROADMAP.md frontmatter has 'last_updated: 2026-05-21' (correct — updated during reconciliation) but the footer reads '**Last updated:** 2026-05-15'. The frontmatter and the human-readable footer are inconsistent. Any reader comparing the two sees contradictory dates.
findings.fix_sketch: Update the ROADMAP.md footer '**Last updated:** 2026-05-15' to '**Last updated:** 2026-05-26'.

findings.title: SECURITY.md footer 'Last updated' says 2026-05-15 despite frontmatter showing 2026-05-21
findings.detail: SECURITY.md frontmatter has 'last_updated: 2026-05-21' but the footer reads '**Last updated:** 2026-05-15'. Same inconsistency pattern as ROADMAP.md.
